trivia_bullets.text: Common from Murders at Karlov Manor (MKM), released February 9, 2024. MKM frames Ravnica through a crime-fiction lens. The Suspect mechanic is one of MKM's four new keywords (alongside Clue, Disguise, Collect Evidence), each drawn from mystery-genre vocabulary.
sources: Wizards.com: Murders at Karlov Manor Mechanics

trivia_bullets.text: "Blasted sketch artist did too good a job. Looks like it's time for me to lie low for a little while." — no named speaker. The line is voiced by the wanted criminal reacting to their own wanted poster visible in the art.

The Suspect keyword gives menace and prevents blocking — a mechanical translation of 'actively evading the law.' Repeat Offender self-suspects for 2B, then rewards a second activation with a +1/+1 counter. CoolStuffInc's MKM Pauper review (Paige Smith) called it 'a good little mana sink for games that go long,' noting that its self-sustaining loop is unusual at common: most counter-putting commons at common require external triggers while this one is purely mana-activated. Pauper-legal and available in Standard at release.
sources: CoolStuffInc: Murders at Karlov Manor Pauper Review by Paige Smith

trivia_bullets.text: Creature type Human Assassin. EDHREC rank 24,816 — minimal Commander adoption.

related_cards.name: Agency Coroner
related_cards.note: Fellow MKM black common that rewards sacrificing a suspected creature for double card draw — direct mechanical synergy

related_cards.name: Basilica Stalker
related_cards.note: Fellow MKM black common with Disguise; shares the identity-concealment / evasion thematic cluster

related_cards.name: Alquist Proft, Master Sleuth
related_cards.note: Lead R.A.M.I. detective; Suspect mechanic is the mechanic opposing forces like the Repeat Offender use
